text: Return of a Jedi, Part I
circa 10 ABY
Jessalyn Valios
Tortured Soul, Corellia
Simon Sezirok, Mira Isaria, Bazil McKenzie, Lehec
New Republic, Jedi, Sith
Months pass before Jessalyn's rescuers return her to the New Republic in an attempt to find a way to awaken the sleeping Jedi Knight, whose soul is caught inside an elaborate Sith Mind Trap.

<SENSORS> SF-5541 enters the system with little pomp or circumstance.%rOne moment the Firespray doesn't exist, the next it does. Momentarily%rstreaking toward the populated planet at extremely fast speed, left over%rfrom the trip through hyperspace, it slows to a lumbering crawl, a thin%rstream of blue coolant ejecting from a gash in its once sleek hull.
<COMMUNICATIONS> From CS-9685 over frequency 5000.00: "SF-5541, this is%rthe Chancellor. Sensors indicate some sort of problem. What is your%rstatus? Over."

<COMMUNICATIONS> From SF-5541 over frequency 5000.00: "Uh..." a shaky%rvoice answers after a moment. The alien accent is thick in a male, human%rvoice. It continues, "This is the... ship..." A pause again and a sound%rof fumbling, then, "Soul Searcher. This is Alvin Davis flying. Heading%rtoward Corellia. This is not a problem, yes?"
<COMMUNICATIONS> From CS-9685 over frequency 5000.00: "SF-5541, this is%rthe Chancellor. Sensors indicate a, err, leak in your ship. Is there a%rproblem? Over."

<COMMUNICATIONS> From SF-5541 over frequency 5000.00: ""Uh..." Again a%rpause and more fumbling. The channel remains open and the man can be%rheard, apparently speaking to himself. He continues after a moment,%r"... leak? Ah, yes. Small leak. Uh... pirates?... yes, I think pirates%ris the word. Pirates attacked us in the last system. We should be okay%rfor landing. And get repairs. Chancellor. Soul Search... uh... out. This%rdevice is a terrible piece of work, Phi-"

<COMMUNICATIONS> From CS-9685 over frequency 5000.00: "<A few moments of%rsilence> ... Uhh, SF-5541, please land in the Chancellor's primary%rdocking bay. We'd like to take a look at your vessel, just to ensure you%rwon't burn up in reentry. Over."

<COMMUNICATIONS> From SF-5541 over frequency 5000.00: "This is the Soul%rSearch," the voice states unnecessarily. A crackling sound can be heard,%rprobably the speaker swallowing before speaking further. "Landing on the%rChancellor is probably more dangerous than landing on the planet.%rAt-moist-fear controls run better, and the controls are sluggish now.%r<The voice gets quieter, apparently as he talks to someone else, but the%rchannel remains open> 'At-moist-fear'? What in the nine hells is that?%rWhat? Oh! <The voice continues normally> Yes, landing on the smaller bay%rmight be dangerous, Chancellor."

<COMMUNICATIONS> From CS-9685 over frequency 5000.00: "Soul Search,%rclose to five hundred units, and then deactivate your engines. We can%rtractor you into the bay with little to no dangerous side effects. We%rhave technical units standing by. Over."

<COMMUNICATIONS> From SF-5541 over frequency 5000.00: "Chancellor," the%rvoice states after a thoughtful silence. "We have no desire to delay our%rtrip to the planet. Are we under some sort of arrest? We know people on%rthe planet that can give us the repairs we need for a price we can%rafford. If you wish to search our ship, can you not do this on the%rsurface? Then we will be able to be about our business without%runnecessary delay."

<COMMUNICATIONS> From CS-9685 over frequency 5000.00: "Soul Search, you%rare cleared to land on the planet," Comes the response after, well, some%rtime, "We have crews standing by in the primary starport, awaiting your%rarrival. They have been notified of your situation and are readily%ravailable to assist you in any way you need. Over."

<COMMUNICATIONS> From SF-5541 over frequency 5000.00: "Thank you,%rChancellor," the voice says, followed by what can only be a relieved%rsigh. "Thank you for your assistance. Soul Search out. See? That was not%rnearly so bad. You should trust me to do the talking more oft-"

The Firespray 'Soul Search', or rather, the Tortured Soul, begins making%rits way toward Corellia. Simon Sezirok, calling himself 'Alvin Davis'%rsettles back in his seat at the co-pilot's station as Phive continues to%rmaneuver the ship.

<SPACE> An Assault Shuttle lifts off from the Chancellor, and sets a%rcourse towards the planet, engines pushing her along quite nicely. After%rdodging through the space lanes, she lies in wait in orbit, in between%rthe Firespray and the planet. Or at least, that's what it might look%rlike.

<SPACE> The Firespray moves slowly and casually toward the planet, and%reven continues on as the Assault Shuttle comes into view, interfering%rwith the course that it was moving toward. Still leaking blue, steaming%rfluids, it slows its crawl pace even further, then begins to angle%rtoward another lane, still pressing toward Corellia.

<SPACE> After the Firespray goes past the Assault Shuttle, she in turn%rspins, and follows the other towards the planet, silently. A few moments%rof tailing her persist, before she coms out into the general vicinity of%rthe pair of ships, <All vessels, please make way for SF-5541. She is%rleaking coolant bad. We are providing escort, in case she requires a%rtow.> And so, she moves out to broadside the other ship, matching her%rcourse and speed down to a dime.

<COMMUNICATIONS> From SF-5541 over frequency 5000.00: "I have a bad%rfeeling about this," comes the thickly accented voice once more, the%rwords obviously directed toward someone other than the com channel. He%rcontinues, "This is the Soul Search. Thank you for the escort, Bazil%rMcKenzie. Beginning landing sequence now."

The Firespray comes down somewhat heavy in the landing port, but whether%rthe poor landing is from an unskilled hand at the stick or ship%rmalfunction, it's difficult to say. A thick, blue liquid drips out a%rgash in the aft section of the ship, making a small puddle.%r____________________________________________________________________%rCoronet Space Port - Coronet City

This port is busy, like most, but here the hot breath of takeoff%rand the deep rumble of engines is closer, more of a feature of the port%rthan an annoyance. The landing pads and small maintenance bunkers sprawl%racross a large open area open to the sky and surrounded by blast walls%rto protect the farmlands beyond. Pilots, engineers and techs, mostly%rnatives, move among the hardware as if at home in the confusing and fast%rpaced Port. A large complex off to the side houses control functions,%rtransport authority offices, and the CorSec station.%r____________________________________________________________________%rSimon%rOf average height and fair coloring, the young man before you%rhas dark brown hair and eyes of a color somewhere between blue and gray.%rHis hair is parted and cut short. His eyes are deep-set, looking more%rready to draw his brow into a deep frown than a warm smile. For facial%rhair he wears a well groomed goatee and mustache, trimmed short and of%rthe same deep color as the rest of his hair. All in all, the man's%rdemeanor can be summed up in a word: intense.%rThe man before you is dressed in earth tones. Light tan, loose%rfitting trousers are tucked into soft leather boots that come up to just%runder his knees, and are tied tight with brown, leather chords. Tucked%rinto the top of his pants is a simple shirt of a matching color. Over%rthis is a loose wool tunic of dark brown, covering his arms completely%rand hanging down below his waste. It's comfortable clothing, suitable%rfor most climates and cultures.%rStrapped diagnolly across his chest and back is what appears to%rbe some sort of harness. It's worn in the way some people wear a%rbandolier, yet there is nothing attached to the device. A long shaft of%rcylinder rises over his left shoulder, a rod sheathed where some%rwarriors sling their sword.%rCurrently, the man's hood is pulled up, concealing his face in%rshadows. With his back bowed slightly, either from a heavy weight or old%rage, it's difficult to tell the man's age. He might not even be human.%r____________________________________________________________________%rFrom the Assault Shuttle, a small crew offloads. A pair of medics, a few%rtechs, and even a few Marines. And, clad in civilian garb, and leading%rthe group outwards, is Bazil McKenzie. The group strolls towards the%rFirespray, and waits there patiently. Turning, Bazil offers towards%rLehec--one of the marines, "And you thought guarding the ship was%rboring." Bazil is armed, in all appearances, as are the Marines. And,%ranyone who could tell, may very well notice the minds of a pair of%rsquadrons of Marines in the Assault Shuttle--waiting for something.
The area around the Firespray is slowly cleared away by port officials,%rjust in case the ship decides to blow up, or something.

Slowly, hesitantly, the man that identified himself as Alvin Davis%rcreaps down the landing ramp of the Firespray that just landed, that%rcontinues to slowly leak hyperdrive coolant onto the warm tarmac below%rit. A staff is in his right hand, a thick length of wood bracketed by%rbrass hoops at its top and bottom. The man moves with the lumbering,%rhunched over motions of an old man, and bobs his cowled head in a series%rof nods as he looks toward the flock of armed marines. At the bottom of%rthe ramp, he stops in his tracks, resting heavily on his staff as though%rthe short trip out of the ship had exerted him.
Lehec gives an innocent look. "It was." he says smiling.

Bazil, himself, just waves the group to stay where they were, save%rLehec, and strolls forward towards the man--Alvin. "Greetings, sir. I'm%rafraid, unfortunately, it seems you know myself, but I'm at a lacking to%rpresume to know you," He extends a hand, "Bazil McKenzie. What can I do%rto help you today? Mind if our techs take a look at your vessel,%rperhaps?" One ear is cocked as his attention wavers for a moment, that%rlittle bug planted in his ear chittering away to inform him of%rsomething, or another. Cold metal arms, and that ear. All fake.
Lehec follows Bazil while closely watching Alvin for any sudden%rmovements.

Over in the far reaches of the spaceport is a small figure sitting on%rthe ground. Her coat is grubby and worn and her appearance is generally%rdirty and unkempt. Sitting next to her is a small jar, which some%rpasserbys (the ones who don't take the long way around to avoid her)%rdrop credits into. She sits motionless on the busy tarmac, seemingly%runaware to everything around her.

Alvin keeps his head down, his hood covering his face entirely in%rshadows, even as Bazil comes over and extends his hand. His eyes look%rdown at the outstretched hand a moment before taking it awkwardly in his%rleft, his own right hand not lessening its grip on his staff. Turning%rswiftly toward the leak, he lumbers toward the puddle. He kneels down,%rsetting his staff beside him and puts both hands into the blue goo. He%rbrings his hands to his face then, looking at them, then moving them%rwithin the shadows of his hood as though smelling them.

Picking up his staff, he stands once more, then turns back to the%rmarines and answers, "I am not one that works on ships. I do not know%rwhat this is. If your... techs... want to look at it, be my guest. If%rthey do not get in the way of what I have come here to do Bazil Mc-...%rMister McKenzie. Sir."

Wiggling his brows at Alvin, Bazil peers behind him, and waves a hand%rtowards the techs. "Your communications--did you have someone else on%ryour ship? Are you, or anyone else injured?" He inquires simply, eyes%rnarrowing even now, as he looks at the man. Something about this was%rfamiliar, and weird. Maybe it was the way he said Bazil's name, or the%rway he held that staff. Or maybe just the way he stooped--he had run%rinto many a bum on many a planet. Perhaps he knew this one. Or perhaps%rnot.

The man calling himself Alvin brings his left hand to his face, rubbing%rhis chin and cheek thoughtfully. The movement causes his cowl to push%rback somewhat from his face, revealing pink flesh that is puffy and%rirritated where around streaks of blue gunk.

"There is one person that I need to take to get medical care, Mister%rMcKenzie. She took injury when the pirates attacked us. If you can%rsimply tell me how to get there, I will be further indebted to you."

A child totters up to the woman sitting immobile in the spaceport, its%rmother obviously not watching where it is going. Thinking that Mira is a%rstatue of some sort, it creeps closer, staring and peering at her. Mira%rcontinues to sit very still, aware of the child and yet seeing no real%rreason to bother with it. This has become a daily exercise for Mira,%rjust sitting and letting her mind drift over the spaceport, trying hard%rnot to forget the skills she was taught by Simon. She had hopes that if%rher mind drifted far enough, it might locate Markus, who had been gone%rfor far too long for it to be a "quick trip to Tatooine" like he had%rsaid. As her mind drifts, she finds her mind drifting to piles of blue%rgoo, a rather strange topic that she had not visited since last week. Of%rcourse, that had been a side effect of her visit to the trash cans%rbehind the Mostlian Steak House. They had some strange side dishes.%rMeanwhile, the child creeps closer.

"You'll want to wash that goo off before it melts through your skin, and%rkills you," Bazil offers simply, waving one of medics behind him%rforward, and grabbing at her little kit of wonders, sifting through it%rfor a cloth, which he proffers forth. "That is hyperdrive coolant. Quite%rnoxious," He offers simply, "I can either provide you with assistance%rwith these medics, or if you'd prefer, the hospital is southwards," He%rmotions.

And then, it comes to him. Him. Simon. "Of course, you'd know that%ralready," He plaintively states, stuffing his hands, now, in his%rpockets. "I'll leave you to whatever business you have," He offers,%r"Unless you require any further assistance from myself or my medics."%rHis business was his own, and not for the likes of Bazil to interfere%rwith. Not anymore, anyways.

Quickly looking back at Bazil, Lehec seems perplexed as to how Alvin%rwould know his way around the port. Thinking no more on it he continues%rhis scan of the surrounding area, chuckling slightly as he watches the%rsmall child aprouch the old woman.

Hyperdrive coolant can be, as many spacers know, a terrible irritant to%rthe skin. The blue gunk that Alvin had meant to smear on his face as a%rquick way of disguise does the trick more soundly than he could possibly%rhave imagined, burning and itching to the point that it is nearly%runbearable. For other people, it probaly is unbearable... but the True%rSource is with him, and fills him from skin to bone. He draws on it to%rcontrol the pain, and keeps his facial features smooth.

Still, the natural desire to scratch overwhelms Alvin to an extent, and%rthe offered cloth tips the scales in his mind. Gritting his teeth behind%ra smile, Alvin accepts the offered cloth, then begins wiping the%runcomfortable substance from his face. The cowl slips back, and when%rAlvin brings the cloth away, a face covered in a red rash, blistered and%rsore, is revealed.

"Some have thought I looked Corellian," Alvin says in response to%rBazil's words. "But I am not. South, you say? Thank you." His eyes move%rto Lehec, and he gives the silent fellow a nod before turning his%rattention to the rest of the starport. Crowded.
Lehec sees Alvin nod and nods in reply all the while making sure that%rthe area stays secure.

All of a sudden Mira's eyes flip wide open and she sits straight up,%rsending the child, who is at this point very close to her, crying back%rto its mother. Though she couldn't quite make the connection, she knew%rthe blue goo was telling her that someone she knew was very nearby.
Simon.

The girl scrambles to her feet, moving almost instinctively in the%rdirection of his ship. Mira pushes her way through the crowded%rspaceport, growing ever closer to the location where Simon's ship is%rparked.

"Some say that a fool is little less than someone who cares not to know%ranything," Bazil replies, "I tend to like knowing who's who in the%runiverse and what their deeds do. Yet there are also those that I care%rnot to know what they do, for it is beyond me, and beyond my class. It%ris they whose business I ignore. I need not pay heed to it, for it is%rindeed theirs and theirs alone, as well as those in their class."

Finally, Bazil ends his philosophical stint, and waves his hand south.%r"Yes," He offers with a smile, "The hospital is to the south. There, you%rcan find whatever medical attention you or your passangers require. Good%rluck, Si--..." A pause, "Alvin." And with that, he turns, and waves the%rstill mingling medics and Marines off to the ship again. The techs,%rhowever, remain, and begin to work at Alvin's vessel.

Turning to Lehec, "Keep and eye on the techs, will ya? I'm gonna go let%rthe crew know to return, and then I'll be back." His eyes dance about%rthe port casually, himself. And then, there's Mira, and his eyes pass%rright over her. Turning to speak to Lehec again, he pauses, turns, and%rlooks at her again. Mira. Who is, now, approaching. One hand strays to%rhis side, near his belt, and he half-grumbles to himself, before%rcatching his hand's intention, and setting off for the girl. This could%rbe, at the least, interesting.
Hearing the child scream, Lehec's eyes dart back to the old woman. What%ris she doing? Why is she coming to the ship. Slowly readies his rifle.%rLooking to Bazil, he say "Yes Sir."

Tension fills 'Alvin' at Bazil's near slip with his name. The man%rrecognized him. Of course, he would. If there was anyone on Corellia%rthat would recognize Simon by voice alone, it'd be Bazil McKenzie. More%rimportant than the recognition, though, was Bazil's reluctance to act on%rit. That was much more important. And surprising.

Drawing in a deep breath, a pin-prick of a sensation touches Simon's%rmind, and he turns his eyes in the direction of the approaching Mira.%rHis blue eyed gaze widens in surprise and dread. How could such a large%rplanet like Corellia be so small as to bring Mira and Bazil to him%rwithin moments of his landing?
Lehec's movement with the weapon catches Simon's attention, and he turns%rtoward the marine. He slowly raises his staff to gesture toward the%rrifle, saying, "That is not necessary, warrior."
Surprised at Alvin's words. Lehec looks towards Bazil as what to do,%rnoticing that something isn't right.

As Mira slowly approaches Simon's ship, her footsteps become slower,%ralmost hesitant. When they had last parted company it hadn't exactly%rbeen friendly. In fact, if she remembered correctly, there were many%rthreats and much anger involved. And she knew that Simon could easily%rfollow through on them if he wanted.

Once Simon's ship is in view, it only takes her a moment to notice Bazil%rthat Bazil has noticed her. And that he is now moving towards her. Her%reyes then flicker over to Simon, who, it seems, has also noticed her.%rThe girl's forward motion carrying her towards Simon's ship comes to a%rstop, and she just stands there, looking between the two men.

"Sir, where are you going? Sir?" Lehec asks urgently. About to reach out%rwith his free hand but remembering what Alvin said about the Blaster%rRifle and Bazil's sudden movements he isn't too sure what is going on%ranymore.

"Mira," Bazil whispers as he approaches the girl, slowing as he comes%rwithin range, keeping himself at least partially between her and Simon.%rOf course, little good would whispering /or/ standing in the way be if%rSimon really wanted his way made. "Go find some shelter. He's here.%rYou'll be safer, if you can find a place to hide," He assures her, just%rin case. He hadn't known what had gone on prior to her leaving, but he%rcould only assume the worst. And, besides, Simon had his mean streak;%rwhat with the Hospital kidnapping and all. And, Cort was an ally of%rSimon's. Or at least, was, meaning not anymore. He really ought'nt to%rhave left his Directorship.
Turning towards Lehec, he offers simply, "Stand down, Private. Just keep%ran eye on those techs." And me, he nonverbalizes.
A little confused but understanding the order, Lehec returns to his post%rwatching the tachs. Every now and then he quickly glances back at Bazil,%rAlvin, and that strange old woman.

Forgetting himself a moment in the surprise of Mira's appearance, Simon%rstraightens momentarily to his normal height, a scowl painting across%rhis blistered, abused face. He blinks then, resuming his stoop as he%rturns his eyes back toward Lehec as Bazil whispers his words to the girl%rthat had been his companion. Was.

None of this mattered, of course. What mattered was still in the belly%rof this ship, waiting to die a slow, waisting death if she were not%rgiven some attention. Using his staff as a walking aid, he moves once%rmore toward the ramp leading into the Firespray, remembering to hide his%rnormal, broad stepped pace in the shorter steps of a feeble, withered%rman. That was supposed to be his disguise, after all.

Lehec was surprised for a moment. He could have sworn that he saw Alvin%rstand straight up....Things seem to just be getting stranger by the%rmoment. Keeping his eyes on Alvin as he slowly walked the ramp onto the%rFirespray, watching and waiting to see if Lehec really did see something%rin that quick moment.

Though Mira had seen Bazil, and heard Bazil, she continues to just stand%rrooted to her spot rather dumbly and stare at Simon, waiting for him to%rdo something. And what he does, is something she fully does not expect.%rHe ignores her. That almost hurt more than any attack he could have sent%rher way. As he walks towards his ship, she turns her gaze to Bazil and%rblinks a few times, not entirely sure what to say. Finally she settles%ron, "What's he doing here?"

"I'm not sure," Bazil responds quietly, "He has someone in his ship,%rthat needs medical attention. And his ship is leaking dangerous fluids,%rwhich my technicians are working on trying to fix now. But I don't know%rwhy he's here, or who he has with him." And with that, he ends it, and%roffers her a weak smile.

After a short while, the one calling himself Alvin strolls back down the%rramp, pushing in front of him a floating stretcher containing the body%rof a beautiful, young woman. Her red hair spills down to her shoulders,%rframing a sleeping, peaceful face. Sitting on the stretcher beside the%rwoman is the staff Alvin had been using.

Pausing at the base of his ship once more, Alvin looks in Bazil and%rMira's direction. They had thus far not interfered, but that could%rchange. Especially now, should Mira see who it was he was tending. She'd%rbeen there when they'd dragged her away, after all.

Mira turns her attention now to Simon's ship and the still growing%rpuddle of blue goo next to it. "Oh," she says, half to the blue goo and%rhalf to Bazil's unhelpful reply. She stares at it for a moment, until%rSimon finally emerges again, this time pushing a stretcher.

"I know her," Mira blurts out almost as soon as she sees the red hair%rtumbling off the sides of the bed. "She's the lazy woman we took from%rthat temple. With Cort. And the bird monster. She sleeps all the time.%rWe had to carry her _everywhere_." The girl shakes her head%rdisapprovingly. She still does not move, however, wary of getting too%rclose to Simon.

Bazil is simply confused at the whole bird monster thing. Maybe Morganna%rtagged along, and she actually decided that nowadays she should wear%rsome sort of outfit that had feathers, and things. So she could pretend%rto fly. And light herself on fire.
Or maybe Mira was crazy.
Regardless, Bazil peers at the new entrance, as well, and blinks, "Who%ris she? Do you know her name?"

Lehec still attempting to figure things out for himself (and giving%rhimself a migraine in the process) looks amazed at Mira and Bazil. He is%rstill unsure of what to do so he pretends to pay attention to the techs%rwhile listening to what is bieng said.

Before pushing further, Simon, still trying to stick to his disguise as%rAlvin, turns his full attention to Lehec. The man seemed to have the%rmovements and stance of a trained soldier. If he was a true warrior,%rthen perhaps he knew something of honor.

"You," he says, addressing the marine. "If these... techs... can be%rtrusted, perhaps you can do me a service and walk with me to the%rhospital? I have heard that this Corellia is like a fallen oak, majestic%ron the outside, yet crawling with vermin on the inside." Before waiting%rfor an answer, he turns a harsh gaze in the direction of Bazil and Mira.%rThey would likely follow along as well, but he wasn't going to invite%rsuch calamity.
"Sir? Should I escort him?" Lehec asks.
Lehec slowly begins to follow next to the stretcher. Walking along with%rAlvin slowly scannning the area and waiting for Bazil to reply as they%rmove.

"I do," Mira says, trying to remember it. It had been so long since she%rhad seen her. "Something like Jessa Lynne Valerie. I remember because I%rhave a cousin named Peggy Lynne Sue on the fourth moon of Valrthros%rNine. Her family is _weird_. They farm tea. In the middle of nowhere."%rMira shakes her head disapprovingly. "Something like that."

Jessa Lynne Valerie? There /was/ a ring to that, but it wasn't very%rmemorable. Pondering it for several moments, he thinks, and thinks. And%rthen that, too, clicks. Something he ran across in one of Luke's vast%rfiles that he didn't destroy yet. Jessalyn, whose last name was still%rescaping his mind. Could that be her? His girlfriend, or something, if%rhe remembered correctly. Hopefully, Simon hadn't defiled her body or%ranything.
"Go with him," He orders to Lehec simply, standing there, peering at the%rnew girl. "Tea, huh?" He offers to Mira, nodding, "I like tea." Tea was%rgood.
"Yes Sir." Lehec replies. "Shall we go then Mr. Alvin?" Feeling rather%runcomfortable about what is occuring but knowing that he has to follow%rorders Lehec begins to move South to the Hospital.

At Lehec's question, Alvin begins walking slowly and surely, pushing the%rsleeping Jessalyn down the street, her stretcher floating at waist%rlevel. Hunching over one end of it as he was, he was able to look at her%rslowly rising and falling chest, relieved that she was still breathing.%rShe had been off life support for a few days now, and the one calling%rhimself Alvin was concerned that she wouldn't make it to another life%rpreserving device.

"Yes, to the hospital," he replies verbally to Lehec. He turns his gaze%rover his shoulder toward Mira, wondering silently if she would follow.%rOr bring trouble, like Markus Lisardis or the Jedi Luke Skywalker.

Mira bobs her head up and down in reply to Bazil's comment about tea%rbeing good. Without thinking about it, she begins to follow after Simon,%rkeeping her distance just in case he remembered the threats he had made%ragainst her the last time they had seen each other. Her hand reaches%rdown to touch the pocket of her robe where she kept the half of the%rlightstaff that Simon had given her, just to make sure it was still%rthere. Despite the thick crowds in this part of the city, ti was easy%rfor the girl to keep track of Simon, even when he was eclipsed by a%rpasserby or a vendor's cart. His familiar presence was so strong and so%rclose that it was like a spotlight to her, though there was something%rthere that there hadn't been before, and it troubled her. A shadow%rlurking underneath, hiding. For now.

After Mira starts to walk away, Bazil grumbles to himself slightly. What%rto do, now? He had told himself not to interfere with their business.%rBut his curiosity was too strong in this to ignore. Starting off to the%rsouth, a few feet behind Mira, he follows them, whispering quietly into%rhis delapidated comlink. No need to be undercautious--especially if%rSimon decided to go postal.

As chance would have it, the very reason Simon had given for asking the%rescort of Lehec presents itself, and in an untimely manner. A hooded,%rgun wielding stranger darts in front of the pushes stretcher, nearly%rcareening into it as he looked over his shoulder in the direction he was%rrunning from. A woman's purse in his hands, he doesn't so much as slow%rdown as he turns sharply, moving at a full sprint toward an alley.%rFollowing the thief's passing with a cool, blue-eyed gaze, Simon looks%rtoward Lehec, expecting the soldier to attend to his law enforcement%rduties. Or perhaps escorting he and Jessalyn was more important than%rcutpurses?

There was a time, not too long ago, that Simon would involve himself in%rthe capture of such criminals. Times had changed, though. These people,%rmired in the sinful nature of machines and technology, would have to%rtake care of themselves. He continues to push Jessalyn forward.

Mira continues to walk casually down the path after Simon and the lazy%rsleeping woman. The sudden appearance of the man, however, causes her%rhand to once again drop down to the lightsaber in her pocket, ready to%rdraw it out should the man attempt to hurt Simon or Jessa Lynne. But he%rdoesn't. He runs off, disappearing rather quickly into the crowds, only%ra faint imprint of his location left on Mira's mind.
Lehec, however, vigilantly decides to persue the pursesnatcher, his%rrifle coming up and firing a number of shots into the crowd, "STOP!%rSTOP!"

Bazil just stares in horror, and continues to follow Mira and Simon. And%rmaybe figure out something. Or find something to do. Or something. Oh%rwell. It was betting than sitting around on his bum and listening to%rJohanna complain, or watching Cricket kill people.
